skill.name: senior-secops
skill.description: SecOps guidance skill with Python CLI scaffolds for security scanning, vulnerability assessment, and compliance checking. It provides reference documentation, target path validation, console reports, and optional JSON report output.
skill.summary: Review security workflows with SecOps CLI scaffolds and reference guidance.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found no malicious intent, prompt injection, network access, credential theft, or cryptographic implementation. Most hits are markdown command examples, generic security wording, or placeholder Python CLI scripts; the remaining risk is limited to user-directed report file writes.

low_findings.title: User-Directed Report File Writes
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with low severity. Each Python CLI script can write JSON output to a path supplied with --output. This is expected report-generation behavior, but users should avoid running it with elevated privileges or overwriting sensitive files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The open and write calls are present at the flagged lines. The operation is user-directed JSON report output with no evidence of hidden writes, persistence, or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The SKILL.md and reference locations are fenced documentation examples or path references, not Ruby backtick execution or code that runs automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are markdown code fences and documentation references. No executable Ruby, subprocess call, shell interpolation, or automatic command execution was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain descriptive SecOps text, JSON content, parser descriptions, or generic reference prose. No cryptographic API, weak hash algorithm, key generation, or encryption logic appears in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no crypto imports or operations in the scripts or documentation. The detections appear driven by security-related words rather than cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Environment File Reference Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. SKILL.md documents copying .env.example to .env as a setup step. No script reads environment variables, opens .env files, or transmits secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a setup command in documentation and a prior report describing that command. No secret access or environment parsing code was found.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Terms Match Legitimate SecOps Context
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. References to anti-patterns, vulnerability assessment, and security scanning match the stated purpose of the skill. No host probing, network scanning, credential collection, or stealth behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms appear in user-facing security documentation. The scripts do not enumerate systems or make network calls, so malicious reconnaissance is not supported by the evidence.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Write API in CLI Report Output
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts use Python file writing for optional JSON reports. This is a low-risk pattern when users control the output path intentionally.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are direct and easy to verify. They are limited to explicit report output and do not indicate malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Security reviews often need repeatable checks and clear reporting. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured SecOps prompts, CLI scaffolds, and reference guidance for review workflows.
actual_capabilities: Provides three Python CLI entry points for security scanning, vulnerability assessment, and compliance checking.
Validates that a user-provided target path exists before running analysis.
Prints a console report with target, status, and finding count.
Outputs JSON results to the console or to a user-selected file path.
Includes reference documents for security standards, vulnerability management, and compliance requirements.
Documents common development, analysis, and deployment commands for security review workflows.
limitations: The Python scripts are scaffolds and do not perform real vulnerability detection yet.
The skill does not perform penetration testing, exploitation, or live network probing.
The reference documents are generic and do not implement named compliance frameworks.
Report output can overwrite files if the user supplies an unsafe output path.

use_cases.title: Prepare review checklists
use_cases.description: Create a structured security review plan before adding real scanner rules or manual checks.

use_cases.title: Prototype pipeline reporting
use_cases.description: Use the CLI scaffolds to test report formatting and target validation in automation workflows.

use_cases.title: Organize control evidence
use_cases.description: Use the reference guides to frame compliance questions and collect review notes consistently.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one project folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Use senior-secops to review the project at [path]. Summarize what the current scaffold can check and what needs manual review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Findings Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use senior-secops to build a security findings plan for [system]. Group checks by authentication, input validation, dependencies, and deployment controls.

prompt_templates.title: Map Compliance Evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use senior-secops to map available evidence for [application] against our compliance requirements. Identify missing artifacts and owner actions.

prompt_templates.title: Design a SecOps Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use senior-secops to design a CI review workflow for [repository]. Include scanner scaffold usage, manual gates, report handling, and remediation tracking.

output_examples.input: Review a backend service before release.
output: Review scope: backend service release readiness.
Current tool support: target validation, placeholder findings, and optional report output.
Manual checks needed: authentication, authorization, input validation, secrets handling, dependency risk, and deployment controls.
Next action: add concrete scanner rules before relying on automated results.

output_examples.input: Prepare compliance evidence for an internal audit.
output: Evidence areas: access control, change management, vulnerability handling, logging, and data protection.
Available materials: generic compliance guidance and report scaffold output.
Gaps: named framework controls, control owners, proof locations, and exception handling.
Next action: create a control matrix and attach evidence links.

output_examples.input: Plan how to add SecOps checks to CI.
output: Workflow draft: validate target path, run scaffold checks, publish report, and require manual review for unresolved risks.
Implementation note: add real detection logic before blocking builds on findings.
Operational guardrail: write reports only to approved artifact directories.

best_practices: Treat the included scripts as starting points and add tested detection rules before production use.
Write report files only to controlled artifact directories in local or CI environments.
Pair automated output with manual review for authentication, authorization, secrets, and deployment risk.
anti_patterns: Assuming the scaffold scripts detect real vulnerabilities without adding analysis logic.
Running report generation with elevated privileges or broad write access.
Using generic reference text as proof of compliance without mapped controls and evidence.

faq.question: Does this skill detect real vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: Not by itself. The included scripts are scaffolds that need real analysis rules before production use.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed scripts do not import network libraries or call external services.

faq.question: Why is the risk level low instead of safe?
faq.answer: The scripts can write report files to a user-selected path, which needs normal file handling care.

faq.question: Can it verify a named compliance framework?
faq.answer: No. The documents are generic and need framework-specific rules and evidence mapping.

faq.question: How should teams use this skill responsibly?
faq.answer: Use it to structure reviews, then add tested checks, controlled report paths, and human approval gates.
